text.title: Service Layer and Async Patterns
text.summary: Async service functions with SQLAlchemy 2.0, paginated queries, and the partial-update pattern with model_dump(exclude_unset=True).

rule.statement: Service functions are async, accept typed parameters, and return ORM model instances or None. Never return raw SQL results or dicts — return the model so the router can serialize via the response schema.
rule.rationale: Returning models decouples serialization from persistence. Routers pass the model to response_model; tests can inspect model attributes directly.

example.code:
from uuid import UUID from sqlalchemy import select, func from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from app.models.user import User from app.schemas.users import UserCreate, UserUpdate from app.utils.security import hash_password
async def get_user_by_id(db: AsyncSession, user_id: UUID) -> User | None: result = await db.execute(select(User).where(User.id == user_id)) return result.scalar_one_or_none()
async def get_users(db: AsyncSession, *, page: int = 1, size: int = 20) -> tuple[list[User], int]: count = (await db.execute(select(func.count(User.id)))).scalar_one() offset = (page - 1) * size users = (await db.execute( select(User).order_by(User.created_at.desc()).offset(offset).limit(size) )).scalars().all() return list(users), count
async def create_user(db: AsyncSession, data: UserCreate) -> User: user = User(email=data.email, name=data.name, hashed_password=hash_password(data.password)) db.add(user) await db.flush() await db.refresh(user) return user
async def update_user(db: AsyncSession, user_id: UUID, data: UserUpdate) -> User | None: user = await get_user_by_id(db, user_id) if not user: return None for field, value in data.model_dump(exclude_unset=True).items(): setattr(user, field, value) await db.flush() await db.refresh(user) return user

antipattern.description: Using synchronous requests.get() inside an async route handler.
antipattern.reason: Sync HTTP calls block the event loop, negating FastAPI's async performance benefit. Use httpx.AsyncClient with async with / await instead.

antipattern.description: Embedding business logic directly in router functions.
antipattern.reason: Router functions with inline validation, DB queries, and conditional logic cannot be tested without the full HTTP stack and are hard to reuse from background tasks.
